Setting Up — Identity
What does this trip need to be?
Select all that apply — trips can be more than one thing. This shapes pacing, density, and tone.
Setting Up — Trip Type
What kind of trip is this?
Tap all that apply — most trips are more than one thing. Romieaux uses every selection to shape pacing, dining, modules, and the trip's name.
Setting Up — Travel Mode
Who are you traveling with?
Pick your core group, then add details below — pets and accessibility work with any selection.
👶
Anyone under 18?
Activates age-specific pacing, dining & activities
🐾
Traveling with a pet?
Activates pet-friendly filtering everywhere
Pet Profiles
Free: pet-friendly filtering across stays, transport, dining. Pet Travel module (paid) unlocks breed-specific airline rules, quarantine regs, and Dogpack integration.
♿
Any accessibility needs?
Adapts stays, transport, activities & pacing
Select all that apply
This adapts your entire trip — accessible rooms, step-free routes, elevator-required stays, adapted transport, proximity to medical facilities, and gentler pacing. All filtering is automatic across every module.
Setting Up — Family
Tell us about the kids.
Ages change everything — nap-friendly pacing for toddlers, adventure activities for teens, kid menus vs. adult dining. Romieaux tailors the entire trip.
What this activates
Set each child's age and Romieaux adjusts everything — pacing, dining options, activity filtering, safety alerts, stay requirements, and scheduling. Infants, toddlers, kids, and teens each get tailored intelligence.
Compatibility Intelligence — Active
Traveling with your partner.
No other platform analyzes travel compatibility. Romieaux maps budget gaps, energy mismatches, and pacing — with prompts to resolve them before the trip.
Setting Up — Group
Bring your people in.
Get your travel companions into the trip now so they can weigh in on destination, budget, and activities — or invite them later.
🔗
Share trip link
romieaux.com/trip/j8k2m...
How it works
They tap your link → create a free account → instantly join your trip. You control what they can do: Planner (builds itinerary), Editor (adds & modifies), or Viewer (votes & views). Your subscription covers their trip intelligence.
YOUR TRIP
What should we call this one?
Based on everything you've told us so far.
Trip Planning — Budget
What feels right for this trip?
Per person, all-in. Romieaux shapes every dining, stay, and activity recommendation around this number.
How many nights?
How are you splitting the stay?
🏡
Sharing a space
One Airbnb, villa, or suite — the total stay cost splits equally across everyone
🛏
Separate rooms
Everyone books their own room — your budget covers your room only
How many people total (including you)?
Total budget per person
$
Setting Up — Engagement Mode
How involved should Romieaux be?
This sets your default experience level. You can change it anytime.
I'll plan it
Free
You're in the driver's seat. Romieaux gives you a starting point, flags conflicts, and surfaces data when you need it — wait times, safety alerts, currency, smart checks. You build and adjust from there.

🔒
Smart Traveler Bundle
Flight price intelligence: best booking window, points/miles vs cash analysis, credit card portal comparison (Chase vs Amex vs Capital One), seat selection strategy, and price drop alerts. Romieaux finds the cheapest route using your existing rewards.
Why this matters
Land at 4pm? Romieaux skips museums and schedules check-in, a short walk, and a dinner reservation at golden hour. Last day adjusts for checkout and departure.

SMART CHECK
R
Trip Smart Check
Catching the small things that derail trips, before they become problems.
ACTIVITIES
🔒
Upgrade to unlock
Your profile already suggests 3 hidden gems for your style. Unlock pacing that flows from morning to night, "skip this — too crowded" alerts, and a way to spot the experiences worth your time.
DINING
🔒
Upgrade to unlock
Restaurants that work for your diet, timed for the right night to book, with "2-hour wait on Saturdays" alerts and the local places tourists never find.
TRANSPORTATION
🔒
Upgrade to unlock
Routes built around how you travel, with live traffic and strike alerts, "take the 8:15 train, not the 9:00" intelligence, and picks that respect your energy.
STAYS
🔒
Upgrade to unlock
Prices compared across every platform, logic like "a villa for 6 is cheaper than 3 hotel rooms," and matches to the kind of trip you're actually planning.
Already booked? Add it here so Romieaux can factor it into your budget, schedule check-in/out times, and set reminders.
SAFETY & PREP
REMINDERS

MEMORY BOOK
Limoncello and Midnight
Limoncello and Midnight
Amalfi Coast · April 2026
8 moments
Tap any to share one
The middle stretch with Sarah and Marcus was the trip. Days 1-2 just us, then the long table at La Sponda, the boat to Capri, Ravello at sunset. Day 7 was perfect — group dinner at Da Adolfo, then the anniversary dinner snuck in at 8:00 like a heist.
10 nights · 1 country · 4 cities · April 2026

Privacy & Data
Connected Apps (Spotify, Google)
FAQ
Discount Profiles
Optional. Enables discount intelligence at attractions, transit, and dining. This data stays private — never shared with trip companions or third parties.
🎖 Age-Based Discounts
50+ — AARP rates, select restaurant discounts
55+ — Many hotel chains, dining (Denny's, IHOP), rental cars
60+ — Museums, transit systems, additional hotel programs
62+ — US National Parks Senior Pass ($80 lifetime), Social Security
65+ — Amtrak, most airlines, Medicare, broadest discount eligibility
Romieaux surfaces discounts at each threshold. You determine which apply to you.
🎖 Military / Veteran
🔒 Encrypted and stored only in your profile. Discount intelligence active for US domestic travel only by default. Auto-suppressed for sensitive destinations. Not visible to trip companions.
🎓 Student
🚗 AAA Member
🏛 Government / First Responder

FAQ
Common Questions
What people ask before they start.
Is Romieaux free to use?
+
Yes. The Explorer tier is free and gives you eight core modules — Activities, Dining, Transportation, Stays, Safety & Prep, Payment Checklist, Memory Book, and Reminders. No credit card required. Add bundles or All-Access whenever you're ready for more.
How does group pricing work?
+
Only the Trip Owner needs a subscription. When you create a trip and invite friends or family, your subscription covers intelligence for everyone on that trip. Invitees create free accounts and get full access to the trip's itinerary, polls, budget alignment, and all active modules.
What's the difference between bundles and All-Access?
+
Bundles ($9.99/mo each) group modules by travel style — Explorer, Smart Traveler, Family, Solo, Couples & Celebrations. All-Access ($29/mo or $299/yr) gives you every module and every engine. If you travel often or with different kinds of groups, All-Access pays for itself quickly.
Can I cancel anytime?
+
Yes. All subscriptions are month-to-month with no long-term commitment. Cancel anytime from Settings. Your data and profile are always yours — they persist even on the free tier. Annual plans can be cancelled with a prorated refund.
Does Romieaux book travel for me?
+
Romieaux is a travel intelligence platform, not a booking engine. It designs your experience, organizes your planning, and links you to book through the providers you already trust. You control where and how you book — Romieaux just makes sure every decision is a good one.
What is My Profile?
+
Your profile is a record of how you travel — your energy level, trip style, budget, dietary needs, music taste, and more. It grows with every trip as Romieaux learns what works for you. Every recommendation is shaped by your profile, not generic data.
Can I travel with pets?
+
Yes. Toggle "Traveling with a pet?" during onboarding to activate free pet-friendly filtering across stays, transport, dining, and activities. The Pet Travel module (Couples & Celebrations Bundle) unlocks deeper intelligence including breed-specific airline restrictions, quarantine rules, and Dogpack integration.
Is my data secure?
+
Security and privacy come first. All data is encrypted in transit and at rest, and we follow SOC 2, GDPR, and CCPA standards. Your profile and trip data are never sold. You can export or delete your data anytime from Settings.
Does Romieaux support accessibility needs?
+
Yes. During onboarding, toggle "Any accessibility needs?" to activate accessible stays, step-free routes, adapted transport, and proximity to medical facilities across every module. Select from wheelchair/mobility device, limited mobility, visual impairment, hearing impairment, sensory sensitivities, or add custom notes. Accessibility filtering is always free — it's never gated behind a paid tier.
Are there senior, military, or student discounts?
+
Romieaux surfaces discount intelligence at attractions, transit, and dining through optional Discount Profiles in Settings. Age-based discounts show tiered brackets (50+ through 65+) so you determine which apply. Military/Veteran status is encrypted, US-domestic only by default, auto-suppressed for sensitive destinations, and never visible to trip companions. Student, AAA, and Government/First Responder profiles are also available.
PRO
For Travel Professionals
Romieaux for Business.
Romieaux's intelligence, in your workflow.
Why it matters
🧠
The Research That Gives You Time Back
Twenty-five modules and eighteen engines work behind every recommendation you make. Hidden gems, wait times, credit card optimization, pacing — things no advisor can research by hand for every trip.
📊
Earn More Per Trip
Build higher-value itineraries in less time. The depth Romieaux brings to every recommendation makes the trip itself more memorable — and worth more.
What you get
👥
Multi-Client Dashboard
Manage every client's trips from one view. Build their travel profile with them or for them — your accumulated knowledge of each client compounds trip after trip, ready when they call again.
🏷
White-Label Itineraries
Your logo, your brand, your client's experience. Every PDF and shared link carries your agency, not ours.
🔌
API Access
Connect Romieaux to your existing tools, CRM, and booking platforms. Built on the open standard for secure AI integrations.
Who it's for
Travel Advisors Hotel Concierge Wedding Planners Corporate Travel Tour Operators Destination DMCs
$99/mo
Unlimited clients. Unlimited trips. Full API access.
Business Verification
Instant approval: Valid IATA, CLIA, or network credential → trial starts immediately.
Independent / no credential: Brief review (24–48hr) of your website or profile → trial activates after verification.
Questions? [email protected]
DISCOVER
Guided Discovery

CREDIT CARD
Active Intelligence · Cards Working For You
$63 saved on this trip.
From the cards already in your wallet.
Best Move · Stays
Le Sirenuse: pay with Chase points, not cash.
Your Sapphire Reserve transfers to World of Hyatt at 1:1, and Le Sirenuse is bookable through Hyatt's Small Luxury Hotels collection. 22,000 points = 3 nights vs $1,860 cash. You'd be paying 8.5¢ per point — well above the 1.5¢ Chase pays out as cash.
✓ Booked via Hyatt · view confirmation
SAVES $340
Best Move · Dining & Activities
Use Sapphire Reserve at restaurants and for travel.
Sapphire Reserve earns 3× worldwide on dining — best in your wallet for restaurants. Flights and hotels booked direct also earn 4× on Sapphire. Across ~$1,400 of dining + trip spend, 4,200 Sapphire points at 1.5¢ transfer value = $63.
EARNS $63 IN POINT VALUE
Quiet Win
Skip the Sapphire portal for the Trenitalia leg.
Trenitalia.com is €18 cheaper per ticket than Chase Travel for the Rome → Naples Frecciarossa, and the rewards math still works on Sapphire. Saves $51 on two tickets.
✓ Booked direct on Trenitalia.com · view confirmation
How This Works
You shared Sapphire Reserve and Amex Platinum during onboarding. Every time a stay, dining, or transport recommendation lands in your itinerary, it's run against your portfolio's transfer partners, current promotions, and bonus categories — and tells you the highest-value way to pay. No card data is stored beyond type and issuer.
PREVIOUS TRIP
Dec 2025 · 7 nights · Solo
🇲🇽 Tulum, Mexico
Reset & Recovery
"The trip that taught me what slow felt like."
Trip Name
Tulum, in Pieces
Total Spent
$2,560
Stayed
Nest Tulum
Cost Saved
$340
Highlights
🏛 Stay
Nest Tulum, jungle-side bungalow. Open-air shower, no AC by choice. Stay Advisor flagged a quieter property over the beachfront big-name resort.
🧘 Wellness
Daily 7am yoga at Sanara. Cenote swim + temazcal ceremony Day 4 — protected as the trip's centerpiece, no other plans that day.
🍽 Dining
Hartwood for the splurge night. Burrito Amor every other morning. The Instagram-famous spots got skipped in favor of where locals ate.
💰 Cost Intelligence
Saved $340 by booking Nest direct vs Hotels.com. Rejected the $180 Chichen Itza day tour for a $40 cenote tour with a local guide — better experience, less crowded.
Memory Book
8 moments · tap any to share
Open full memory book →
Reflection
"I came home not relaxed exactly — that's not the right word. Settled. Like something that had been spinning had finally stopped. The protected Day 4 with no plans was the move. I'd come back for that day alone."

35 Candles, One City

Over Budget
$80
Stayed
Hôtel Particulier
Intelligence Saved
$520
The milestone splurge. Alex's 35th in Paris went $80 over the original budget — a single Day 4 dinner at L'Ami Jean was the deliberate overage. Even with that, Intelligence Savings of $520 left this trip net-positive at $440.
Highlights
🏛 Stay
Hôtel Particulier in Montmartre — five rooms in a hidden mansion. Stay Advisor chose this over the Bristol for the privacy and the garden breakfast.
🍽 Dining
Septime for the birthday night (booked 4 months out). Du Pain et des Idées for breakfast. Frenchie was flagged as overrated for the price — skipped it.
🎭 Activities
Private after-hours Musée Rodin tour. Picnic at Square du Vert-Galant at golden hour. No Eiffel Tower ascent — Alex had been before.
💰 Cost Intelligence
Saved $520 routing the Hôtel Particulier through Amex FHR (free breakfast + room upgrade). Skipped the €180/pp Bateaux Mouches dinner cruise for a quieter Seine walk instead.
Memory Book
8 moments · tap any to share
Open full memory book →
Reflection
"Alex cried at Septime. Not the food — the menu had a handwritten note that had been arranged weeks ago, just '35, with you.' I'd forgotten about it until it landed. Best birthday I've ever planned for anyone."

Rio, by Ear

Highlights
🏨 Stay
Hotel Fasano Ipanema. Beach-facing corner room, the rooftop pool with the Sugarloaf view. Solo Female Travel layer flagged the safest neighborhoods and best entrances at night.
🎭 Culture
Sambadrome night during Carnival. MAR museum at sunrise. Live samba at Pedra do Sal on a Monday night. Activity Curator scoped out the right venues for a solo woman traveling alone.
🍽 Dining
Two tasting menus — Lasai and Oteque — with chef\'s counter seats both nights. Confeitaria Colombo for breakfast. Aprazível for sunset over the city.
💰 Cost Intelligence
Saved $420 booking Hotel Fasano direct vs the OTA price. Routed all dining through a Brazilian Real card to skip the 3% FX spread that Visa would have charged on the tasting menus.
🚺 Solo Female · Active On This Trip
3 design decisions kept you safer in Rio.
Hotel Fasano (gated property) chosen over Santa Teresa Airbnbs. Sugarloaf at first light, not evening. After-9pm rides routed through 99 verified drivers in Lapa & Centro.
View →

Reflection
"Four nights and I think the city rewired me. Not in a dramatic way — just a recalibration. The way the music spilled out of doorways. The way no one ever rushed. The captain\'s wife who invited me back. I\'m already looking at flights for Carnival next year, with friends this time."
COST INTELLIGENCE
Active Intelligence · Trip-Wide Cost Optimization
$454 saved across 3 decisions.
Every booking weighed against alternatives — points vs cash, stay vs villa, ferry vs charter.
A digest of every cost decision Romieaux made for this trip. Tap any item to see it in context — nothing is duplicated, every detail lives in its home module.
Stays
$280/night avoided
Le Sirenuse over Villa Fraulo
CHOSEN
Villa Fraulo would have saved €280/night ($2,800 across the trip), but added a 45-minute daily commute from Ravello. For an anniversary trip with limited days, walkability won.
Tradeoff: walkability vs cost
See full Stays comparison→
Flights
$340 saved · points strategy
Le Sirenuse: pay with Chase points, not cash
SAVES $340
Sapphire Reserve transfers 1:1 to World of Hyatt · Le Sirenuse bookable through SLH collection. 22,000 points for 3 nights vs $1,860 cash. You'd be paying 8.5¢/point vs 1.5¢ cashback baseline.
See full points strategy in Credit Card→
Activities
2 decisions weighed
Capri boat charter over public ferry
PREMIUM
Public ferry would have been €22/pp (€44 total) vs €400 charter. The charter wins for the anniversary day — Grotta Azzurra access, no waiting in line, swim stops you choose. The trade is intentional, not an oversight.
See activity reasoning→
Ravello concert split 4 ways
CHOSEN
€180 total · €45 each · paid by Marcus, settled in group balances. The Ravello Festival is the anchor group experience — only €5/pp more than the alternative free belvedere visit, with live Bollani Trio jazz instead of just the view.
See in Group Coordination→
Dining
$96 earned in points
Amex Platinum at restaurants only
EARNS $96
Sapphire Reserve earns 3× worldwide on dining — best in your wallet for restaurants. Flights and hotels booked direct earn 4× on Sapphire. Across ~$1,400 of trip dining + travel, that's 4,200 Sapphire points at 1.5¢ transfer value = $63.
See full card strategy in Credit Card→
Transport
$51 saved · skip the portal
Trenitalia direct, not Chase portal
SAVES $51
Trenitalia.com is €18 cheaper per ticket than booking the same Frecciarossa through Chase Travel, and the rewards math still works on Sapphire as a direct travel charge. Two tickets, $51 total saving.
See full points strategy→
SITA bus available — but rejected
PREMIUM
SITA bus from Positano to Ravello is €4/pp vs €40 private car. Bus rejected for Day 5 because of the Bollani concert end at 11:30 PM (last bus is 9:45 PM). Private car holds for late-night return, all 4 travelers.
See Smart Check Day 5→
Settlement
FX routed via Wise
Wise for cross-border, Venmo for domestic
CHOSEN
Sarah → Marcus and Jordan → Marcus both go through Wise (NYC → London) — cheaper FX than Venmo international. Sarah → Alex stays Venmo (NYC ↔ NYC). Same corridor, same rail.
See payment routing in Group Coordination→
How This Works
Cost Intelligence is a digest, not a separate engine. Each decision lives in its home module — Stays, Credit Card, Group Coordination, Smart Check. This view aggregates them so you can see every cost trade-off in one place. The trip total isn't a magic number; it's the sum of decisions you can audit one by one.
INSURANCE
Active Intelligence · What You Already Have
You're more covered than you think.
Don't pay for what your cards already do.
Already Covered
Trip cancellation up to $10,000.
Your Sapphire Reserve covers cancellation if you or your travel companion gets sick, hurt, or has to cancel for one of 13 covered reasons. Both flights and Le Sirenuse are eligible because you booked them on the card.
Already Covered
Trip delay reimbursement after 6 hours.
If your flight delays push past 6 hours or require an overnight stay, Sapphire reimburses up to $500 per ticket for meals, lodging, and essentials. No claim form headache — just save receipts.
Already Covered
Primary rental coverage in Italy.
If you decide to rent a car for a day trip down the coast, decline the rental company's collision waiver — Sapphire's primary coverage applies in Italy and most of Europe. Saves roughly €25/day.
Gap Worth Filling
Medical evacuation isn't included.
Sapphire's emergency medical coverage tops out at $2,500 — fine for a clinic visit, not enough for an evacuation home. For a 10-day trip with boat days and cliffside terrain, a standalone evacuation rider runs $18 for the trip and covers up to $500K. Worth it.
What This Saves You
Most travelers buy a $89–$240 trip insurance bundle for a trip like this without realizing 80% of what they're paying for is already in their wallet. You're paying $18 for the one piece that actually matters.

Live Event Match · Day 5
🎼
Strong match · Your jazz preferences
Ravello Festival — Stefano Bollani Trio
Piano jazz under the stars at Villa Rufolo's belvedere — overlooking the gulf of Salerno. The Bollani Trio is one of Italy's defining jazz acts, and their setlist leans into the Italian standards that match your listening history.
Friday · 9:30 PM · €45 per seat · Day 5 of your trip
How This Was Built
Your Spotify history, the music you logged during onboarding, and the country you're visiting — All three were blended into a soundtrack that fits the rhythm of this specific trip. Live events are pulled fresh from Ticketmaster and Bandsintown for your travel dates.
AIRPORT & TRANSIT
Active Intelligence · From Door to Door
JFK to Le Sirenuse, mapped.
Lounges, walk times, bags, ground transport.
JFK Departure · Terminal 8
Greenwich Lounge · 14 minutes from your gate.
Your Amex Platinum gets you into the Greenwich at JFK — quieter than the Centurion, full bar, real plates, hot showers. Walk time to gate B41 is 14 minutes at a relaxed pace, so you can stay until boarding starts.
PRIORITY PASS · CENTURION · GREENWICH · ALL ELIGIBLE
Bags · Tracked End to End
Two AirTags paired. We'll watch them.
Both checked bags are tracked through AirTag plus the American Airlines baggage API. You'll see live carousel assignment at FCO before you land, and a notification the moment your bag is loaded onto the connecting transfer to Naples.
LOST BAG PROTOCOL READY · ESSENTIALS STORES MAPPED
Arrival · FCO to Positano
Private transfer beats the train by an hour.
After an overnight flight, the Frecciarossa to Naples plus a Caremar ferry adds 90 minutes of station hopping with luggage. A pre-booked car (€280, splittable) is door-to-door in 3.5 hours and gets you to Le Sirenuse in time for the golden hour walk Romieaux scheduled. Already booked.
CAR CONFIRMED · DRIVER SPEAKS ENGLISH
Jet Lag Plan
Sleep on the plane. Sun on arrival.
Six hours ahead, eastbound — your circadian rhythm wants to shift forward. Try to sleep the second half of the flight, then push through to a 10pm Italian bedtime on Day 1. Day 2 was paced light on purpose so you're not pushing through fog at sunrise yoga.
What's Watching In The Background
Flight delays, gate changes, security wait times, lounge capacity, baggage handling, ground transport pricing. All of it is being watched — and will only ping you if something changes that needs your attention.
INTENTIONALITY
Why This Trip · Anchored
To come back whole.
The reason underneath the reason.
Your Intention
"This trip is about slowing down enough to remember what we sound like together — without screens, without schedules, without the next thing waiting."
Captured during planning · Anchors every recommendation on this trip.
Daily Reflection · Optional
A short prompt at the end of each day.
Day 3's prompt: "What surprised you today that you didn't expect to notice?"
Day 7's prompt: "If this slowness is what you need more of, what at home would have to change?"
How It Shapes the Trip
Your intention is why Day 2 has only one scheduled activity. Why dinner reservations sit at 8:30 instead of 7. Why Day 6 has nothing planned at all. This whitespace is defended from itself — every time it considered adding "one more thing," your intention won.
SHOPPING & OUTFITS
Built From Your Closet
10 looks. Nothing new to buy.
Your wardrobe, mapped to your itinerary.
Day 1 · Arrival & La Sponda Dinner
The cream linen midi + your tan slides.
La Sponda is "smart elegant" but the terrace is candlelit and the night air on Positano cliffs runs cool. Your linen midi reads romantic without being formal, the slides survive the cobblestone walk down from Le Sirenuse, and the cashmere wrap you packed last winter in your closet handles the breeze.
ALL ITEMS ALREADY OWNED
Day 2 · Capri Boat Day
The black one-piece, the linen shirt, the straw hat.
Four hours on water, sun direct from 10 to 2. Your structured one-piece flatters in photos and dries fast for the Grotta Azzurra swim. The men's-cut linen shirt thrown on top means no sunburn on the boat ride back. Pack the straw hat — Capri's afternoon sun is no joke.
ALL ITEMS ALREADY OWNED
Day 5 · Ravello Festival Concert
The navy slip dress + flat sandals.
Open-air piano jazz at Villa Rufolo. The slip dress photographs beautifully against the gulf-of-Salerno backdrop and the gardens at golden hour. Flats not heels — the path from the parking down to the belvedere is uneven stone.
ALL ITEMS ALREADY OWNED
The Only Gap
You don't own grip-sole sandals.
Positano's stairs are notoriously slick after morning humidity and your closet has sleek leather and rubber-flat slides — neither will hold on those steps. Three options were found at your budget at boutiques on Via Pasitea, walkable from Le Sirenuse. This is the only purchase you actually need to make.
Why This Works
You connected your wardrobe through Alta when you onboarded. Your closet was read, cross-referenced it against the itinerary's dress codes and weather, and built every look from clothes you already own. Travelers who use this module pack 40% lighter and buy 70% less on trips.
SUSTAINABILITY
Lower Impact · Same Experience
Six swaps already in place.
None of them required compromise.
Stays
Le Sirenuse runs on 100% renewable electricity.
Two equally-rated hotels that don't disclose energy sources were filtered out. Le Sirenuse also sources 80% of its kitchen ingredients from within 50km — which is part of why the food is the food.
Transport
Trenitalia Frecciarossa, not the regional flight.
Rome to Naples by high-speed rail produces roughly 4kg of CO₂ per passenger. The same trip on a regional flight produces 47kg. The train is also faster door-to-door once you account for security and transfers — which is why Romieaux scheduled it that way regardless of the carbon math.
Dining
Three reservations are Slow Food affiliated.
La Sponda, Lo Guarracino, and the Ravello family farm tour all source from regional producers and follow Slow Food guidelines for biodiversity and fair labor. The food is better because of it, not despite it.
Activities
The boat charter operator runs a marine cleanup program.
This charter was chosen over two cheaper options because the operator partners with Marevivo on Mediterranean plastic recovery. Half a percent of every booking funds the cleanup work along the coast you'll be sailing.
The Footprint
1.8 tons of CO₂ for the full trip.
That's 22% lower than an unfiltered version of the same trip. The flights are the largest single component (1.4 tons) and the part that's hardest to reduce. The trip can be offset the trip through Gold Standard verified projects for $24.
How This Works
Sustainability isn't a separate filter you turn on. It's baked into how every recommendation is scored. The trips are built to be quietly lower-impact than the trips you'd build yourself — without ever asking you to choose between the experience and the planet.
TRANSLATION
More Than Words
Italian, with the context that matters.
Phrases, gestures, and the things tourists get wrong.
The Phrases You'll Actually Use
Buongiorno · Buonasera
bwon-JOR-no · bwon-ah-SAY-rah · "Good day" before lunch, "good evening" after
Un tavolo per due, per favore
oon TAH-vo-lo pair DOO-ay · "A table for two, please"
Il conto, per favore
eel CON-toh · "The check, please" — they will not bring it unprompted
Era squisito
EH-rah skwee-ZEE-toh · "It was exquisite" — the compliment that lands
The Things Tourists Get Wrong
Don't order a cappuccino after noon.
Italians treat cappuccino as a breakfast drink — milk after a meal is considered hard to digest. Order an espresso, a macchiato, or a caffè after lunch. This was flagged for La Sponda specifically because tourists order cappuccinos there constantly and the waitstaff are too polite to say anything.
"Ciao" is informal. Use "Salve" with strangers.
Ciao is for friends. With shopkeepers, hotel staff, and anyone older than you, "Salve" is the warm-but-respectful greeting that does not read as overly familiar.
Camera Translation · Always On
Point your phone camera at any menu, sign, or label. Translation happens in place — including handwritten chalkboards at family-run trattorias. Works offline once you've downloaded the Italian language pack (already done for you when you set the trip).
Medication Identification
Coming Soon
Forgot a medication? Scan what's at the pharmacy.
Point your camera at any pharmacy box abroad. Romieaux identifies the active ingredient, what it's commonly used for, and matches it to the brand name you'd recognize at home. If you've listed medications or allergies in your profile, you'll see soft flags — for example, a warning if the box contains ibuprofen and you take blood thinners.
Romieaux is not a healthcare provider. Identification is informational. Always confirm with a licensed pharmacist before taking any medication.
Available Now · Find a Pharmacist
Need a pharmacist who speaks English in Positano? Romieaux maintains a vetted list of local pharmacies near your stays, including which ones are open late and which staff speak your language. One tap to call, message, or get directions.
What's Different Here
Google Translate gives you words. You get context — when to use which phrase, what gestures land badly, and what tourist mistakes the locals notice but don't correct. Built from the ROAM Network's reports on what travelers wished they'd known sooner.
CLIMATE & CARBON
Destination Risk · Real-Time
The Amalfi Coast is changing.
What's Being Watched for your dates.
Conditions · April 14–24
Within historical norms.
Average highs of 65–72°F, low rain probability, sea temperatures still cool for swimming but warm enough for the boat charter. April is one of the most stable months on this coast — Romieaux chose the dates partly for that reason.
Pattern Deviations Watching
The Tyrrhenian Sea runs warmer than usual this year.
Mediterranean sea surface temperatures have run 1.5–2°C above the 30-year average for the past three springs. For your trip this means slightly warmer water for the Capri swim, and a higher chance of brief afternoon thunderstorms forming over the cliffs in late April. The 10-day forecast is being watched — we will move the boat day if needed.
Trip Footprint
1.8 tons of CO₂.
The flights account for 1.4 tons. Ground transport is 0.2. Stays and dining together are roughly 0.2. This is 22% lower than an unfiltered version of the same trip — the Frecciarossa-over-flight choice and the Le Sirenuse renewable energy sourcing do most of the work.
Lower-Carbon Alternative
If you wanted to cut the trip in half: 0.9 tons.
A 7-day version that swaps the JFK→FCO direct for a JFK→LIS→NAP routing on the airline with the highest fuel-efficiency score, plus skipping the Capri boat charter, drops the trip footprint to under 1 ton. Same coast, different shape. Romieaux can build it as a side-by-side if you ever want to see it.
Why This Module Exists
Climate change is reshaping where and when it makes sense to travel. Destinations that were reliable five years ago — Greek islands in summer, Venice in winter, southern Spain in late spring — are now genuinely unpredictable. The deviations are watched so you don't have to.
WELLNESS & HEALTH
Quietly Looking After You
Recovery built into the rhythm.
So you actually come back whole.
On The Itinerary · Already Booked
Le Sirenuse spa · Day 1, 4:30 PM.
A 90-minute traditional Italian pijat massage on arrival day — the 6-hour time difference plus an overnight flight means your body is going to land with cortisol elevated and circulation tight. The massage is the recovery move, not the indulgence move. Included with your room.
Quietly Adjusted For You
Day 3 has nothing scheduled before noon.
The Capri boat day on Day 2 is physically more demanding than it looks — sun exposure, balance work on water, salt and dehydration. Day 3's first activity was pushed to 12:30 PM so you can sleep, hydrate, and not feel chased into the next thing. This is what pacing means in practice.
Pre-Trip · Already Done
Vaccinations checked. Prescriptions packed.
No additional immunizations required for Italy beyond your routine boosters — Romieaux verified against the CDC and WHO advisories for your travel dates. Your daily prescriptions cleared customs review (one of yours is regulated differently in the EU, so you have the documentation if anyone asks). Refills set to be available in Naples through a partner pharmacy if anything goes missing.
If Anything Goes Wrong
English-speaking care, mapped before you need it.
Three English-speaking physicians within 15 minutes of Le Sirenuse. The closest emergency-capable hospital that takes international patients is Ospedale Costa d'Amalfi, 22 minutes by car. Your Sapphire Reserve travel insurance plus the evacuation rider you added in Insurance covers up to $510,000 in medical costs. You'll never need to look any of this up — it's in the app, ready, in a moment when searching Google from a foreign hospital bed is the last thing anyone wants.
Why This Module Exists
Wellness travel isn't only spa retreats and yoga certifications. For most travelers, it's the quiet work of *not getting depleted* by the trip you came to enjoy. Recovery is scheduled into pacing, verifies your medications, maps medical care before you need it, and connects all of it to the insurance you already have. For travelers planning around specific health goals — detox programs, dental work abroad, marathon training, mental health recovery — this module unlocks deeper functionality including accredited facility discovery and pre-trip medical clearance coordination.
SOLO FEMALE
Active Intelligence · A Layer Across Every Decision
3 design decisions kept you safer this trip.
Romieaux made these on your behalf — without making the trip feel cautious.
Past Trip · Rio · Feb 2025
The same module ran on your Rio trip.
Different city, same intelligence layer. Below: 3 design decisions Romieaux made on your behalf in Rio.
Travel Mode · Your Choice
Affects what shows below.
"Both" — Safety intelligence + community visible. The default.
Decision 1 · Stay
Bisma Eight chosen over Villa Mathis (cheaper).
Single secured entry, female night concierge, in-house drivers. Open villa compounds rejected as a category for solo nights alone.
+$70/night
Decision 2 · Itinerary
Campuhan Ridge moved to 6:30am sunrise.
Recommended afternoon timing rejected — the trail is unmonitored after dark and you'd descend in low light. Sunrise version is busier, safer, and the photos are better.
No cost
Decision 3 · Transport
After-10pm rides routed to Bluebird only.
Gojek/Grab still default for daytime. After 10pm, the app silently switches to Bluebird dispatch (vetted, metered, traceable). You don't have to remember.
~+$3/ride
All three decisions made silently, surfaced here for transparency.

What This Looked Like · Day 3
"Driver tried to drop me 200m from the hotel. The app caught that his plate didn't match what was sent. I cancelled. Next driver was Bluebird. I never had to figure it out — the app just made it the right answer."
— A real moment, captured by the module's protocol checks. This is what the safety layer is for.
Solo Risk Profile · Ubud & Seminyak
Overall: Low-moderate
Daytime: Low
After 10pm: Moderate
Ubud is one of Southeast Asia's safest destinations for solo women — low violent crime, a long history of solo traveler tourism, strong spa/wellness infrastructure. Primary risks are non-violent: motorbike theft, opportunistic theft from unlocked villas, taxi scams, and unwanted attention in crowded markets. Seminyak at night (clubs, beach bars) is the only area where standard big-city caution applies.
Where You Are · Where You're Not
After dark — the map of yes and no.
✓
Central Ubud (Monkey Forest Rd, Jl. Hanoman)
Walkable until ~11pm. Well-lit, tourist-dense, shops open late. Your stay is 6 minutes from the main strip.
!
Seminyak beach strip (Jl. Kayu Aya)
Fine until 10pm with a group. Solo after 10pm, take a Bluebird taxi (not Gojek, not walking). Ka Beach Club and Potato Head maintain strong security.
✕
Kuta after 11pm
Skipped from your itinerary entirely. Aggressive street vendors, drink-spiking reports, known pickpocket zone. Nothing there worth the risk profile for a solo woman after late evening.
✕
Rice paddy walks between villages after sundown
Beautiful during the day, unlit and unmonitored after dark. The Campuhan Ridge Walk (your Day 3 plan) is scheduled for 6:30am sunrise — not after dark.
Why Bisma Eight · The Safety Math
Not the cheapest option. The right one.
Three Ubud stays scored higher on Booking.com for nightly price. Here's why Bisma Eight was chosen for a solo woman on a reset trip:
✓
Single secured entry. One lobby, 24-hour staff, key-card elevator to your floor. You don't walk through an open courtyard to your room at 11pm.
✓
Female concierge on night shift. The desk is staffed by Wayan (9pm–7am) — verified, English-speaking, rated 4.9 for solo female guest support.
✓
In-house transport arrangements. Bisma's driver network is vetted, flat-rate, and accountable. No negotiating on dark streets with unknown drivers.
✓
Restaurant and spa on property. Dinner alone does not require leaving the hotel on your most depleted nights.
✕
Skipped Villa Mathis ($180/night). Individual villa in a rice-paddy compound, single shared front gate, no night staff. Nicer in daytime photos — harder at midnight alone.
Transport Protocols · By Time Of Day
6am–
6pm
Gojek or Grab — ride-share apps
Cheap, GPS-tracked, driver photo and plate verified in-app. Share ride status to your trip companion (or Romieaux's check-in system) with one tap.
6pm–
10pm
Gojek OK, but verify plate before entering
Confirm plate, driver name, and destination shown on screen match before getting in. If the car does not match, don't enter — cancel and rebook. The $2 cancellation is irrelevant.
10pm–
2am
Bluebird taxi (the blue ones with "Bluebird Group" logo)
The one reliable, metered, traceable taxi company in Bali. Call +62 361 701 111 for dispatch, or request one from the hotel. Do not enter any other taxi that says "Bluebird" — there are clones. The real ones have the logo ring on the roof and a driver ID card visible on the dash.
Never
Street-hailed taxis or "transport, miss?" offers
The most common solo-female tourist issue in Bali is meter-rigging or off-route driving. Uber-priced, hard to dispute, and occasionally worse. The 3 minutes you save isn't worth it.
Quiet Check-In · Your Choice
Nightly check-in
Marisa (your sister) gets a "safe" text at 11pm. If you miss 2 check-ins, she gets a call.
Location sharing with Marisa
Live location visible only to her, only during this trip. Turns off automatically when you land home.

Cultural Context · What's Normal vs. What's Not
Bali is a Hindu-majority island in a Muslim-majority country. The intersection creates specific social codes you'll want to read correctly:
Male attention at markets ≠ threat.
Vendor calls ("Hello miss, where you from?") are sales, not propositions. Politely smile and say "Tidak, terima kasih" (no thank you) and keep walking. This is normal commerce, not harassment.
Temple dress code is strict — and enforced.
Shoulders and knees covered. Sarongs are provided at most temple entrances. Menstruating visitors are traditionally asked not to enter temples — honor this even if not asked.
Eye contact with men — measure the room.
In Ubud/Seminyak tourist zones, normal. In rural villages or traditional warung settings, brief eye contact and a nod is enough. Sustained eye contact can be misread as interest.
Red flag behavior to exit from.
Drivers or guides offering "special tour" to a remote location. Anyone insisting on carrying your bag "to help." Drink orders appearing at your table unprompted. Trust the instinct — you can leave without explaining.
Why This Module Exists
Solo female travel is the fastest-growing segment in global travel — and the least well-served by mainstream booking platforms, which treat safety as a warning badge rather than a design layer. Romieaux's Solo Female module is integrated into every upstream decision: accommodation selection weighs solo-specific safety criteria, transport booking enforces verified providers at night, neighborhoods are pre-annotated by hour, and community is verified identity-first. The goal isn't to make you cautious — it's to make the cognitive work of staying safe disappear into the background, so you can actually rest.
